text:
Ava Chen: Kicking off Nebula-13B v1 onboarding. Aim: pick a quant plan (int8 vs fp8 vs gguf) and run smoke + sensitivity. Primary workload: chat; secondary: codegen. Constraints: p95 latency <120ms on ded pool, quality delta vs fp16 <= 5%.
Ravi Patel: Sounds good. Priority is chat latency/quality. Embeddings can be deferred. Who owns the prompt suite?
Marta: I will. I'll assemble chat_regress_50, codegen_smoke_25, and redwood_eval_100. Light-weight prompts first to get quick signals.
calib-bot: Calibration job submitted: nebula13b:v1 -> int8(gguf) on pool-gpu-a. Job id c-8732. Estimated runtime 2h.
Jonas: Quick decision point: weight quantization per-channel symmetric or per-tensor? I recommend per-channel symmetric for stability; activations run both ABS-mean and KL.
Ava Chen: Per-channel for weights agreed. Activation probes: test KL, MSE, and bias-correction toggle. Add a smoke acc...

text:
Ethan Park: FYI I hit a reproducible divergence in the new int4-qkernel during a long-context smoke: mismatch appears when absolute position indices exceed ~100k.
Sofia Li: Can you share the repro command and a small log?
Ethan Park: repro: `python test/long_kv_check.py --model builds/int4-alpha --prompt long/100k.txt --dump-meta`
Ethan Park: log snip:
[2026-02-11 09:14:02] PREFILL tokens=100128
[2026-02-11 09:14:02] KV_WRITE pos=100128 shard=1 hdr.pos_lo=46768
[2026-02-11 09:14:03] KV_READ reconstructed_pos=46768 (expected=100129)
Rohan Desai: hdr.pos_lo looks truncated; likely compact header using 16-bit. Is this new packing introduced in qkernel v2?
Ethan Park: yes, the qkernel team packed header to save memory. FP16 path not affected.
Sofia Li: Quick mitigation: runtime flag `kv.header_mode=full32` to force 32-bit positions for canary. Add unit test: for x in [0, 200000]: assert(u...

title: #eng-ml: 2141234567
preview:
Ava Chen: quick brain dump — I'm prototyping a hotstart heuristic for safetensors sharded models. aim = cut p99 cold-start by ~2-3x for interactive routes. idea: score shards by (estimated token-impact, in-memory size, recent-access probability) and prefetch top-K. open to tradeoffs.
Ravi Patel: nice. can you share ...
text:
Ava Chen: quick brain dump — I'm prototyping a hotstart heuristic for safetensors sharded models. aim = cut p99 cold-start by ~2-3x for interactive routes. idea: score shards by (estimated token-impact, in-memory size, recent-access probability) and prefetch top-K. open to tradeoffs.
Ravi Patel: nice. can you share the scoring formula you're thinking? also worth calling out manifest vs index-first tradeoffs for discovery.
Ava Chen: sketch:
```
priority = alpha * token_impact + beta * access_freq - gamma * size_bytes
where token_impact ~ sum(prob(token in early positions) * param_count_per_shard)
K is dynamic based on free_gpu_mem fraction
```
Ravi Patel: hmm token_impact might be noisy for new prompts. maybe use a warmup bucket for embeddings+first transformer block always higher weight. also fallback policy: if index not found, try manifest -> fallback to sequential shard list.
Jin P...

title: #eng-ml: 1965000000
preview:
Maya Patel: Quick sanity check — bringing up qint8 reranker (rerank-model-qint8-v2) on the prod holdout and seeing overall NDCG@10 drop ~1.8% vs fp16. Split by query length shows a bigger hit on long queries. Anyone else see a length-dependent regression?

Owen Li: Noted. What's the holdout? global shuffle or time-b...
text:
Maya Patel: Quick sanity check — bringing up qint8 reranker (rerank-model-qint8-v2) on the prod holdout and seeing overall NDCG@10 drop ~1.8% vs fp16. Split by query length shows a bigger hit on long queries. Anyone else see a length-dependent regression?

Owen Li: Noted. What's the holdout? global shuffle or time-based?

Maya Patel: time-based holdout last 7 days. Holdout id `holdout_2026-03-14`. Eval command:

```bash
python eval_rerank.py --model rerank-qint8-v2 --holdout holdout_2026-03-14.json --split-by query_length --buckets "0-32,33-128,129-512" --metrics ndcg@10,mrr
```

Maya Patel: Quick paste of bucketed diff (fp16 -> qint8):

```
0-32 ndcg@10 0.726 -> 0.718 (-1.1%)
33-128 ndcg@10 0.642 -> 0.613 (-4.5%)
129-512 ndcg@10 0.588 -> 0.507 (-13.8%)
```

Ben Carter: Whoa large drop for 129-512. Could be tokenization/truncation differences or KV-cache alignment issue during quantiz...

```

title: #eng-ml: 1898765432
preview:
Ravi Patel: Quick follow-up from the precision-profiles RFC — we need a concrete SDK contract for how clients express precision preferences and what to expect from runtime fallbacks.
Sofia Kim: +1. Customers keep asking 'if I ask for FP8 what guarantees do I get vs INT8/4bit'. we should be explicit.
Ava Chen: Can we...
text:
Ravi Patel: Quick follow-up from the precision-profiles RFC — we need a concrete SDK contract for how clients express precision preferences and what to expect from runtime fallbacks.
Sofia Kim: +1. Customers keep asking 'if I ask for FP8 what guarantees do I get vs INT8/4bit'. we should be explicit.
Ava Chen: Can we map it to a small set of fields? e.g. `precision_preference`, `quality_floor`, `latency_target_ms`, `allow_fallbacks` — then runtime negotiates.
Jamal Ortiz: That seems reasonable. Two edge cases: (1) model incompatibility (2) hot fallback during overload. Need to define semantics.
Sofia Kim: For (1) return an immediate structured error if the model binary doesn't support requested precision, unless `allow_fallbacks=true`. For (2) runtime can switch to a fallback precision but must emit telemetry and a `precision_change` event in the response header.
Ava Chen: Example sket...

title: #eng-ml: 1811112222
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Heads-up — did an ember-liteq int8 canary this morning, and we're seeing a small but consistent class of funcall failures (parsing errors) on multi-tool flows.
Ben Carter: Ugh, can you paste an example log?
Alice Nguyen: Pasted sample below — pattern is trailing partial NDJSON / orphaned `,` fragments ...
text:
Alice Nguyen: Heads-up — did an ember-liteq int8 canary this morning, and we're seeing a small but consistent class of funcall failures (parsing errors) on multi-tool flows.
Ben Carter: Ugh, can you paste an example log?
Alice Nguyen: Pasted sample below — pattern is trailing partial NDJSON / orphaned `,` fragments attached after the funcall payload. Only shows up when streaming is enabled + small batch sizes.
Alice Nguyen: ```
{"tool":"search","args":{...}}
,
{"tool":"open_url","args":{...}}
````
Carlos Ruiz: So the model outputs an extra comma + mini-object after the expected JSON? That's weird. Any chance it's streaming boundary cut?
Alice Nguyen: Exactly. It looks like the streaming chunk boundary sometimes delivers an extra chunk that contains a leftover `,
{` snippet. Parsers then treat the stream as ndjson and choke.
Dana Li: @Alice can you share the canary config (batch, strea...

title: #eng-ml: 1769991111

```json

 "model": "redwood/transcoder-v1",
 "prompt": "Summarize...
text:
Ava Chen: Quick Q about the generation contract — how should we guarantee behavior when a stop sequence and max_tokens fight each other? Seeing a case where `logprobs` are null when the stop appears to trigger mid-token. Example request I used below:
```json
{
 "model": "redwood/transcoder-v1",
 "prompt": "Summarize: ...",
 "max_tokens": 1,
 "temperature": 0.0,
 "top_p": 1.0,
 "stop": ["

"],
 "logprobs": 5
}
```
Any established contract for what gets returned (text, logprobs, truncated indicator) when stop detection, tokenizer boundaries, and max_tokens interplay? :thinking:

Rafael: short answer: stop checking is done against the decoded candidate string after each token step (we do not match inside a token bytewise). so if a stop spans token boundaries you might emit the last token before we detect the stop. that can make the returned text appear to contain the stop or part of it d...

Hassan Mirza: +1. right now in our prototype ...
text:
Aditya Rao: hey folks, quick q on Cost Model what-if endpoint. Optimize wants to add a param for `cache_warm_rate` (0..1) so we can model “cold start” vs “mostly warm” prefix/KV cache. otherwise ROI for caching recs is super misleading for customers with bursty traffic.

Hassan Mirza: +1. right now in our prototype we assume warm cache after N requests, but that’s basically hand-wavy. would be much better if what-if can take a warm-rate or “expected hit ratio” so we can present savings bands.

Jonas Weber: to clarify: not asking for full simulator. just a single knob that scales cache-hit contribution in the breakdown. ex: warm_rate=0.2 means only 20% of eligible tokens get the cache discount.

Ethan Park: concern: adding an unconstrained knob makes the API non-deterministic in practice (and easy to game). we’re trying to standardize a small set of canonical inputs that reflect real o...

title: #eng-ml: 1768474332
preview:
Aditya Rao: hey folks — for Optimize what-if endpoint, we need a way to express quantization uncertainty + quality guardrails in the response. Right now we just return `estimated_cost_usd` delta and it’s… misleading.

Jonas Weber: +1. at minimum we should not output a “savings” number without saying “this assumes qu...
text:
Aditya Rao: hey folks — for Optimize what-if endpoint, we need a way to express quantization uncertainty + quality guardrails in the response. Right now we just return `estimated_cost_usd` delta and it’s… misleading.

Jonas Weber: +1. at minimum we should not output a “savings” number without saying “this assumes quality holds” and what we used to decide that.

Arjun Desai: quant is not deterministic across workloads. even for same model + profile, quality hit depends on prompt distribution / max new tokens / tool usage. if we return a single number, product will over-index.

Dr. Maya Srinivasan: we need two separate concepts:
1) *cost/latency uncertainty* (from perf variance, batching, cache, etc)
2) *quality risk* (from quantization/profile change)
Quant primarily affects #2, though it also affects #1 due to kernel selection.

Hassan Mirza: what are we thinking schema-wise? somethin...

title: #eng-ml: 1768099003
preview:

Sana Farid: Specifically for v1 metrics (refusal rate, format adherence, rubri...
text:
Sana Farid: Trying to sanity check what we want to recommend as *default* sampling rates + min sample sizes for “stable” deltas in shadow experiments. Folks keep asking “is 0.1% enough?” / “how many requests until I can trust the chart?”

Sana Farid: Specifically for v1 metrics (refusal rate, format adherence, rubric score avg). Any guidance we can put in docs + Console tooltips that isn’t total hand-waving?

Selene Huang: +1. We should give a rule-of-thumb, but also be explicit it depends on baseline + effect size.

Selene Huang: For proportions (refusal rate / format pass rate), back-of-napkin: to detect a delta of ~1 percentage point with 95% confidence you need *a lot* of samples, esp when baseline is mid-range.

Selene Huang: I’ve been using: if baseline p ~ 0.1–0.3 and you want to detect d = 0.01 (absolute), you’re in the tens of thousands per variant. If d = 0.02, more like low...

title: #eng-ml: 1764456129
preview:
Sana Farid: We need to lock down stats requirements for gate pass/fail. What counts as “significant regression” and what’s min sample size? Right now we have a bunch of hand-wavy “-1% ok” language in docs.

Jonas Weber: +1. Also: are we doing p-values or CI bounds? I’d prefer CI-based non-inferiority over “p<0.05” g...
text:
Sana Farid: We need to lock down stats requirements for gate pass/fail. What counts as “significant regression” and what’s min sample size? Right now we have a bunch of hand-wavy “-1% ok” language in docs.

Jonas Weber: +1. Also: are we doing p-values or CI bounds? I’d prefer CI-based non-inferiority over “p<0.05” given variance + reruns.

Selene Huang: Agree. P-values get weird w/ repeated eval runs + multiple metrics. CI + margin is more interpretable for product.

Karthik Iyer: Can we separate:
1) decision rule (pass/fail)
2) minimum N / power
3) handling flake / reruns
We’re mixing these.

Sana Farid: Yep. Proposed decision rule (per suite): compute per-prompt paired delta candidate-baseline, bootstrap 10k, take 95% CI for mean delta. Fail if lower bound < -M (regression margin). Pass if lower bound >= -M. Otherwise “inconclusive” => rerun / expand N.

Jonas Weber: Paired bootstra...

title: #eng-ml: 1764440011
preview: Jonas Weber: circling back on the “minimal quality spot-check” for perf runs (perf suite + perf-canary). we need to lock a tiny must-not-break prompt set this week so Rina can wire it into the harness. goal is *not* to score quality, just catch decoding/format regressions (e.g., tool call JSON breaks, stop seq chang...
text:
Jonas Weber: circling back on the “minimal quality spot-check” for perf runs (perf suite + perf-canary). we need to lock a tiny must-not-break prompt set this week so Rina can wire it into the harness. goal is *not* to score quality, just catch decoding/format regressions (e.g., tool call JSON breaks, stop seq changes, weird empty output).

Jonas Weber: constraints reminder:
- no prod prompts / no customer text
- must be stable across model families (llama/qwen/mistral)
- should run in <2 min per model per environment
- default decode settings should be “greedy-ish” so it’s reproducible

Claire Dubois: +1. also pls keep it “binary” where possible (parse ok, non-empty, contains required keys, etc.). anything subjective will be noisy.

Rina Kobayashi: what size are we thinking? my vote: 10–12 prompts total, split across chat/tool/long-context. if we do more it’ll start fighting the perf...

title: #eng-ml: 1758888888
preview:
Priya Desai: Heads-up — I'm exploring a lightweight "quant-sidecar" approach to bridge FP8 <-> INT8 model variants at runtime. Thinking it'll let us avoid large runtime changes for a migration window. Thoughts?

Jin Park: interesting. do you mean an out-of-process translator that rewrites weights/activations on the ...
text:
Priya Desai: Heads-up — I'm exploring a lightweight "quant-sidecar" approach to bridge FP8 <-> INT8 model variants at runtime. Thinking it'll let us avoid large runtime changes for a migration window. Thoughts?

Jin Park: interesting. do you mean an out-of-process translator that rewrites weights/activations on the fly?

Priya Desai: Yeah. Two modes: 1) offline stage where we generate a compact delta/adapter for model shards, 2) an in-flight shim that applies conversions for hot requests. The runtime talks to sidecar via tiny rpc.

Mateo Alvarez: main win is we don't have to change kernel scheduling in the serving runtime, right? sidecar takes on quant-format mapping complexity.

Priya Desai: Exactly. Runtime sees same tensor shapes/addresses. Sidecar exposes a stable contract: convert(request_meta, KV_payload) -> compatible_blob. Keeps runtime oblivious.

Sami Noor: tradeoffs?

Priya...

preview:
Ethan Park: spun up fennel-embed-v1.1-small in canary this morning. topline: overall recall@10 ~-1% vs prod, but recall@1 on very short contexts (1-3 tokens) down ~8%. :frown:
Maya Patel: interesting, is pooling change involved? v1.1 switched from mean-pool->attn-pool right?
Ethan Park: yes, we introduced a lightwei...
text:
Ethan Park: spun up fennel-embed-v1.1-small in canary this morning. topline: overall recall@10 ~-1% vs prod, but recall@1 on very short contexts (1-3 tokens) down ~8%. :frown:
Maya Patel: interesting, is pooling change involved? v1.1 switched from mean-pool->attn-pool right?
Ethan Park: yes, we introduced a lightweight attention pooler to boost long-contexts. seems like short contexts lost signal.
Liam Chen: any token norm diffs or embedding table scale shifts? short queries often dominated by single token norms.
Ethan Park: ran a quick token-norm diff vs prod: mean L2 per-token up ~12% for top 20 short tokens. uploaded CSV to artifacts: https://artifacts.redwood/fennel/token_norms.csv
Rina Das: did you also test CLS token fallback? sometimes atten-pool weights CLS less for tiny inputs.
Ethan Park: good point. ran a small comparison: mean-pool recall@1=42.3%, attn-pool=36.1%, cls-toke...

text:
Alice Nguyen: heads up, onboarding hf/serac-slim-7B via convert_hf_to_redwood. Getting frequent early terminations in streaming—responses cut off mid-sentence.

Ben Carter: can you paste the model config + tokenizer special tokens? usually stop logic mismatch there.

Alice Nguyen: here's config.json excerpt and tokenizer special tokens:
```json
{"eos_token_id":50256, "pad_token_id":0, "model_type":"gpt2"}
```
```py
# tokenizer.special_tokens_map
{'eos_token': '', 'additional_special_tokens': ['<|endoftext|>', '<|pad|>']}
```

Maria Gomez: ah — multiple eos-like strings mapped in conversion. Redwood streaming stop checks decoded text chunks for stop sequences, so if tokenizer has both '' and '<|endoftext|>' you can get partial matches and premature stop. Also fast-tokenizer vs python one can split differently.

Ben Carter: to repro quickly, use the SDK streaming call and set stop=[""]....

Arjun Desai: early deltas from the profile library runs are in :bar_chart: (tl;dr good savings, one red flag)

Arjun Desai: model: `rwi/llama3-70b-instruct` (hosted A100-80G + H100-80G)
profile candidate: `qpl_llama3_70b_int8_w8a8_v0.4` (smoothquant-ish; calibration: `calset-2025-02-10-mix1`)

Arjun Desai: aggregate vs baseline (FP16) on our standard prompt sets (decode len ~256, context p50 ~1.5k):
- cost/token: -17.8% (A100), -14.9% (H100)
- p95 latency: +2.3% (A100), +1.1% (H100)
- throughput: +9–12% depending on batch mix

Arjun Desai: BUT: we’re seeing a quality regression on one prompt set: `tools-json-strict-v3`.
- pass@1 strict JSON validity drops ~3.6pp (baseline 96.4% -> 92.8%)
- failure mode: more trailing commas / missing closing brace, mostly on multi-tool outputs

Jonas Weber: thx. is that outside Selene’s thresholds for that suite?

Selene Huang: yeah, for `tools-json-s...

title: #eng-ml: 1738885126
preview:
Selene Huang: trying to pin down what we mean by a “quality regression alert” for route experiments. we need something we can defend + implement w/o full stat-sig in GA. can we align on trigger conditions + how it shows up in Console?

Jonas Weber: +1. we have eval score streams per {experiment_id, variant_id} from ...
text:
Selene Huang: trying to pin down what we mean by a “quality regression alert” for route experiments. we need something we can defend + implement w/o full stat-sig in GA. can we align on trigger conditions + how it shows up in Console?

Jonas Weber: +1. we have eval score streams per {experiment_id, variant_id} from eval harness, but we need: (a) baseline definition (control?) (b) normalization (scores across suites) (c) min sample size / windowing.

Olivia Grant: reminder: the current hook gives us per-prompt outputs w/ a score vector (one per eval rubric) + an overall “quality_index” we can compute. but “overall” is still a product choice.

Dr. Maya Srinivasan: my preference: make “quality regression” conservative + explainable. no p-values. do thresholding on a normalized aggregate + require minimum coverage. also avoid calling it “bad model” — it’s “quality signal suggests regressi...

title: #eng-ml: 1738461022
preview:
Aditya Rao: We’re starting to see nice $/1M output token wins from the new quant profiles, but I’m nervous we’ll “win cost” and silently regress output quality. Can we tie `quantization_profile` to a quality guardrail hook (evals / canary) so we don’t ship surprises?

Quentin Harris: +1. Right now `quantization_prof...
text:
Aditya Rao: We’re starting to see nice $/1M output token wins from the new quant profiles, but I’m nervous we’ll “win cost” and silently regress output quality. Can we tie `quantization_profile` to a quality guardrail hook (evals / canary) so we don’t ship surprises?

Quentin Harris: +1. Right now `quantization_profile` is just a tag in request metadata. No semantic meaning beyond “what runtime requested.” We need an authoritative mapping: (model_id, model_version, quant_profile) -> “guardrail suite + thresholds + last pass timestamp”.

Nadia Rahman: From telemetry side: we can emit quant_profile reliably (ENG-8425), but we can’t explode cardinality with freeform values. If we’re going to hook guardrails, we should enforce an allowed-value list and version it (e.g., `fp16`, `int8_w8a8_v2`, `gptq_int4_v1`, etc). Then eval system can key off it.

Aditya Rao: Exactly. Ideally:
1) Every q...

title: #eng-ml: 1736372044
preview: Marissa Cole: hey folks — need to lock down what “quality” means in Console for route experiments. We keep saying “quality metric” but it’s fuzzy. For beta we need something we can defend + explain in UI. Can we align today on: (1) what eval score(s) we surface, (2) normalization (so it’s not random 0.73 vs 0.81), (...
text:
Marissa Cole: hey folks — need to lock down what “quality” means in Console for route experiments. We keep saying “quality metric” but it’s fuzzy. For beta we need something we can defend + explain in UI. Can we align today on: (1) what eval score(s) we surface, (2) normalization (so it’s not random 0.73 vs 0.81), (3) drift / when to invalidate.

Jonas Weber: +1. From eval harness POV we can emit a few different things:
- per-prompt scores (raw)
- per-suite aggregate (weighted avg)
- task breakdown (format adherence, correctness proxy, safety, etc)
Question is what becomes the single number in Console.

Selene Huang: also pls define if the number is absolute or “delta vs control”. If it’s absolute, customers will assume it’s comparable across routes/models which it won’t be.

Olivia Grant: for integration we already have a planned stream like:
`{route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, ev...

title: #eng-ml: 1733337777

Sara Li: Yep — small table below (rough...
text:
Sara Li: Quick snapshot from last night's micro-bringup for nova-lite-9b. Ran int6/int8 and a hybrid-fallback variant. TL;DR: int6 cuts cost ~42% vs fp16 but we saw a small hit on long-form summarization recall (esp >800 tokens).

Dan Cho: can you paste the perf/cost numbers?

Sara Li: Yep — small table below (rough averages from 10k sample). Note latency is p95 on our test harness.

Sara Li: ```
model | cost/unit | latency p95 | long-recall @800+
nova-lite-9b-fp16 | $1.00 | 210ms | 0.82
nova-lite-9b-int8 | $0.62 | 230ms | 0.80
nova-lite-9b-int6 | $0.58 | 260ms | 0.76
hybrid(int6->fp16 fallback)| $0.63 | 215ms avg | 0.80
```

Maya R.: That p95 jump on int6 is concerning for chat flows. where's the latency coming from? kv-cache misses?

Mateo Alvarez: int6 currently forces a slightly different kernel path that doesn't use optimized fused attention for seq>512 — we hit a slower fallback...

Ava Chen: Yep. priority custo...
text:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — we're planning a 5% staggered rollout of model v5.1-int4-nf4 (bridge variant) tomorrow 10:00 PT. Want to lock the stability gates and auto-fallback thresholds before we flip.
Jordan Patel: Slices we care about? priority customers + longform doc summarization, right?
Ava Chen: Yep. priority customers (acct tier: enterprise), longform (>1024 tokens), and short-chat. Also a generic random sample slice for baseline.
Mike Torres: Quick summary from today's staging shadow run: overall MRR +0.6%, p95 latency +12ms, error rate flat, hallucination rate +0.9 percentage points on longform slice. Small sample though (~6k requests).
Sophia Li: Thanks Mike. That hallucination bump on longform is the main concern. We should set an auto-fallback if hallucination increases by >1.0pp or if MRR drops by >1.5%. Also consider p95 threshold at +30ms absolute.
Jordan Patel: Agree. Need ...

title: #eng-ml: 1719509999
preview: Aisha Khan: quick follow-up on the precision explainability RFC that landed this morning — we still need to decide ownership and the deprecation/playbook for old quant profiles. main reqs i think: per-response explainability fields (quality_delta estimate, profile_id, fallback_reason), stable header for client compa...
text:
Aisha Khan: quick follow-up on the precision explainability RFC that landed this morning — we still need to decide ownership and the deprecation/playbook for old quant profiles. main reqs i think: per-response explainability fields (quality_delta estimate, profile_id, fallback_reason), stable header for client compatibility, and a migration cadence. who wants to own the playbook?

Ben Liu: do we expect the runtime to compute the `quality_delta` at inference time or should that be produced offline by eval and attached via profile metadata?

Clara Martinez: my leaning is runtime-provided, but only an estimate. we can use cached calibration maps produced offline + a small runtime heuristic. example config sketch: 

```
precision_profile:
 id: "fp8-lite-v2"
 explain_mode: runtime
 explain_fields: ["quality_delta_est","fallback_reason","profile_id"]
 expose_headers: ["X-Quant-Profile","X-Q...

title: #eng-ml: 1718801234
preview:

Diego Ramos: Can you repro deterministically? sounds like an index drift in the resume path.
Marta Liu:...
text:
Marta Liu: Quick sanity check — onboarding llamaX-7b-int8-64k shows a context-window handoff issue when resuming from a saved cache around token ~48000. Cold run matches eval, resumed run diverges after ~3 tokens.
Diego Ramos: Can you repro deterministically? sounds like an index drift in the resume path.
Marta Liu: Yep, deterministic seed 42. Repro steps below, very small script. Cold vs resumed outputs differ starting right after resume.
Marta Liu: ```
# repro (run on staging with model build 2026.03.10-rc3)
python tools/gen.py --model llamaX-7b-int8 --max-tokens 64000 --seed 42 --snapshot out/snap.bin --save-snapshot
python tools/gen.py --model llamaX-7b-int8 --resume out/snap.bin --seed 42 --max-tokens 64005 --out out/resumed.json
python tools/gen.py --model llamaX-7b-int8 --max-tokens 64005 --seed 42 --out out/cold.json
python tools/compare.py out/cold.json out/resumed.json --met...

title: #eng-ml: 1718249999
preview:
Priya Sinha: hey folks — seeing a weird CI pattern: when contributors push a quick follow-up commit the GH Actions -> Buildkite bridge sometimes spawns two eval runs that both upload artifacts named the same. one of the runners then errors trying to download the artifact (collision). anyone seen this?
Ben Carter: ye...
text:
Priya Sinha: hey folks — seeing a weird CI pattern: when contributors push a quick follow-up commit the GH Actions -> Buildkite bridge sometimes spawns two eval runs that both upload artifacts named the same. one of the runners then errors trying to download the artifact (collision). anyone seen this?
Ben Carter: yep saw that last week. looks like webhook debounce + retry creates 2 workflow runs with different run_ids but same artifact path.
Sam Nguyen: we added a debounce in the GH side but Buildkite triggers can still be retried by the actions runner. retries + auto-retry for failed uploads = duplicate artifacts.
Priya Sinha: right — failure mode is: older job finishes upload later and overwrites the artifact while newer job already started pulling -> checksum mismatch and eval fails. not great for PR gating.
Luis Rodriguez: quick mitigation: include the workflow_run.id or run_id in...

title: #eng-ml: 1717778000
preview:
Ravi Singh: Quick sync - we need clearer rollback/hysteresis rules for low-bit rollouts. current 'fail fast' reverts are noisy for transient noise.
Olivia Park: Agreed. My proposal: add a hysteresis window so a single spike doesn't auto-revert. e.g., require 3 consecutive windows (1m windows) breaching threshold bef...
text:
Ravi Singh: Quick sync - we need clearer rollback/hysteresis rules for low-bit rollouts. current 'fail fast' reverts are noisy for transient noise.
Olivia Park: Agreed. My proposal: add a hysteresis window so a single spike doesn't auto-revert. e.g., require 3 consecutive windows (1m windows) breaching threshold before revert.
Mei Tan: +1. Also add a cool-down after a revert to avoid flip-flop (e.g., 30m hold).
Jordan Blake: We should separate metrics that trigger immediate safety revert (safety-critical degradation) vs those that are soft (quality delta). Immediate metric: safety_token_mismatch or hallucination_count above X -> immediate revert. Soft metric: human-eval proxy (degradation_score) -> hysteresis.
Ravi Singh: Good. Concrete thresholds suggestion: safety metric -> immediate. quality metric -> breach if degradation_rate > 0.6% for 3 windows. latency -> breach if p95 delta >...

title: #eng-ml: 1711401234
preview:
Ethan Park: Heads-up idea: for Dedicated/Private customers we should sign quant profiles (profile provenance) so runtime can verify origin and prevent accidental unsafe overrides.
Nina Gomez: signing sounds good for compliance. What would we sign exactly? whole profile blob or a canonical digest?
Ethan Park: canonic...
text:
Ethan Park: Heads-up idea: for Dedicated/Private customers we should sign quant profiles (profile provenance) so runtime can verify origin and prevent accidental unsafe overrides.
Nina Gomez: signing sounds good for compliance. What would we sign exactly? whole profile blob or a canonical digest?
Ethan Park: canonical digest + metadata (author, calibration_version, signature_time). Keep blobs in artifact store; runtime fetches signed manifest.
Lukas Weber: runtime cost: verifying a signature per-deploy is cheap, but per-request would be too slow. verify at load-time and cache.
Nina Gomez: also need an audit trail: who approved the profile, which eval anchors passed. tie to ML-rollback playbook.
Ethan Park: proposal: profile lifecycle: draft -> signed-for-staging -> staging-eval -> signed-for-production. Each sign step requires lint + anchor checks.
Lukas Weber: deploy-time checks: cer...
